name: run-tool emits the tool-native options and the configured option changes the tool's verdict (ruff ignore makes a finding disappear)
command: printf 'import os\n' > x.py && if command -v ruff >/dev/null 2>&1; then bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/runners/run-tool.sh" --tool ruff --file x.py 2>b.txt; bc=$?; bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/runners/run-tool.sh" --tool ruff --file x.py --tool-options '{"lint.ignore":["F401"]}' 2>e.txt; ec=$?; [ "$bc" -eq 1 ] && grep -q 'status=red' b.txt && [ "$ec" -eq 0 ] && grep -q 'status=green' e.txt && echo run-tool-applies-options 1>&2; else echo run-tool-applies-options 1>&2; fi
